title: François Bérnarde [004/100]
description: When I walked around some less crowded parts of Venice, I met a man with a remarkable fashion style on the bridge "Ponte San Lorenzo". We started some small talk after having a brief interaction with people on a gondola riding underneath the bridge. He asked me in French what languages I spoke, so I replied that I prefer English. His face and hair also looked distinctive, so I felt confident that I wanted to have him in my project. I explained my intention to take a photograph of him, and he agreed. Meet François Bérnarde. François comes from Boston, Massachusetts. He had come to Italy to attend a wedding in Milano that had just taken place. After visiting Venice, he was going to travel to Florence, Barcelona, and Perpignan in France, where he is going to look for real estate, as he has plans to move to France. François works as a home and garden designer, and as a life and fitness coach. In his client work, he follows a holistic approach that addresses various aspects in combination, like health, hapiness and spirituality. He is educated in several fields, such as classic dance, fashion design, and economics. Actually, he even had designed the clothing that he was wearing. However, he considers fashion design as a hobby rather than an economical chance in these days. You can find out more about François on his website: mindfulbodywellness.com We first took a few photos directly on the bridge where we met. He was not at all in a hurry, and he even made own suggestions. Upon his request, I took a photo of him with some flowers on a balcony in the background. Then I suggested to move a couple of meters to a square, the Campo San Lorenzo, where we would have low sunlight. We first took some photos on the square. As he was very patient and relaxed, it was also a good opportunity for me to technically try out some new things. We finally looked for a nice background, and decided for a greenish door. I took a few photos of his profile with a serious expression, and I felt that this was it. Our session from the first photo to the final result lasted 15 minutes, including some intermittent conversations. I'd like to add that François actually prefers to see himself in a more relaxed, natural pose, and with a softer facial expression than in this photograph. Nevertheless, he gave me permission to select my favorite, as he doesn't want to get his personal self-concept too much involved. I selected this photo, as I find the thoughtful facial expression actually interesting, and due to technical details: the background, the colors, and the sharp contour line of the profile. Thank you very much, François, for participating! This is my 4th submission to the Human Family Group. To view more street portraits and stories visit The Human Family. This picture is #004 in my 100 strangers project. Find out more about the project and see pictures taken by other photographers at the 100 Strangers Flickr Group page.

title: A Venetian Couple Dressed Up for Carnevale
description: This couple was dressed to impress, entirely in gold and cream colors. I think they were moon deities. People dress up in expensive, tailored costumes during Carnevale in Venice, then they walk the streets and canals with an aura of mystery. The traditional quality of the costumes can make it seem like you've gone back a few hundred years in time. Photo by Stephanie Abdul of Clever Fox Web Design.

title: 2013.05.26.119 VENISE - Sestiere di Castello - San Giovanni in Bragora
tags: church architecture italia venise église italie 2013 vénétie sangiovanniinbragora venzzia sestièredicastello
description:
L'église San Giovanni in Bragora est située dans le sestiere de Castello dans la Contrada San Zuane in Bragora. La façade donne sur le campo Bandiera e Moro, et son flanc sud sur le Campiello del Piovan.
Sa fondation remonte en l'an 829. La légende veut que San Magno, évêque d'Oderzo, qui avait fui l'invasion des Lombards (639), avait vu en rêve Dieu lui-même et que ce dernier lui avait donné l'ordre de construire huit églises, dont deux sont nommées d'après saint Jean et une San Zaccaria. Le tout premier écrit qui mentionne cette église date de 1090, avec paraphe du prêtre Andrea Martinaci. Elle a été reconstruite au IXe siècle sous le doge Pietro III Candiano, pour accueillir des reliques présumées de saint Jean élémosinaire, et à nouveau en 1178. Quand Pietro Barbo, succédant au pape Pie II est devenu pape sous le nom de Paul II (1464), l'église qui l'avait baptisé a été restaurée dans le style gothique tardif, sous la forme que nous connaissons aujourd'hui. Le travail a duré trente ans, de 1475 à 1505. C'est à cette date quelle a été consacrée, comme le montre la date à l'avant, sur le linteau de la porte d'entrée.
Le pape Paul II en 1417 puis Antonio Vivaldi en 1678, y ont été baptisés.
Dans une chapelle de l'église, le corps intact de saint Jean le Miséricordieux y est vénéré.
Une distribution de reliques de la vénérable Serafina Gregoris y a été organisée en 2012.

title: Church Of San Lorenzo, Venice - Explored
tags: venice blackandwhite bw italy panorama mexico nikon europe d90 churchofsanlorenzo
description:
This is the Church Of San Lorenzo in Venice, Italy. It's being restored by the Mexican government who will use the site to display the best of Mexican art and architecture over the next 9 years.

The day after taking this the SD card in my camera decided to die. There were about 800 shots on it, of which I managed to salvage around 200. The other 600 were of our tour around the Adriatic, but luckily the wife had her camera with her, so we should still have some holidays snaps!

This is a 14 shot panorama stitched together with Photoshop.
